---
title: Plugins
description: Extend cache behavior with the plugin pipeline.
icon: Puzzle
---

## What are plugins?

Plugins are middleware functions that wrap the adapter's operations. They are applied in order and can intercept reads, writes, and deletes.

```ts
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  plugins: [namespacePlugin({ prefix: "app" }), statsPlugin()],
});
```

## Available plugins

| Plugin | Subpath | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| Namespace | `/plugins/namespace` | Prefix all keys |
| Serialize | `/plugins/serialize` | Custom serializer (superjson, msgpack) |
| Encrypt | `/plugins/encrypt` | Encrypt values at rest (AES-256-GCM) |
| Stats | `/plugins/stats` | Track hit/miss ratio via `cache.stats()` |
| Stale | `/plugins/stale` | Stale-while-revalidate (stub — currently pass-through) |
| Dedupe | `/plugins/dedupe` | Deduplicate concurrent `get` calls for the same key |
| Prefetch | `/plugins/prefetch` | Prefetch related keys on cache hit |
| Audit | `/plugins/audit` | Callback hooks for logging, metrics, or compliance |

## Namespace plugin

Prefixes all cache keys. Prefer `cache.namespace()` for runtime namespacing — this plugin is for when you need the prefix baked into the plugin pipeline at creation time.

```ts
import { namespacePlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/namespace";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  plugins: [namespacePlugin({ prefix: "v1" })],
});
```

The separator between prefix and key is always `:` (e.g. `v1:mykey`).

## Serialize plugin

```ts
import { serializePlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/serialize";
import { superjsonSerializer } from "@justwant/cache/serializers/superjson";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  plugins: [serializePlugin({ serializer: superjsonSerializer() })],
});
```

Supports `Date`, `Map`, `Set`, `BigInt` and other non-JSON types via SuperJSON or MessagePack.

## Encrypt plugin

Encrypts every cached value using AES-256-GCM. Requires a 32-byte key — derive one from `@justwant/crypto/derive-key`:

```ts
import { encryptPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/encrypt";
import { deriveKey } from "@justwant/crypto/derive-key";

const key = deriveKey(process.env.CACHE_SECRET!, "salt", "cache", 32);

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  plugins: [encryptPlugin({ key })],
});
```

`deriveKey(secret, salt, info, length)` uses HKDF-SHA256 and returns a `Uint8Array`.

## Stats plugin

Exposes a `cache.stats()` method that returns hit/miss counts and latency percentiles:

```ts
import { statsPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/stats";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  plugins: [statsPlugin()],
});

const stats = cache.stats?.();
// → { hits: 42, misses: 8, hitRate: 0.84, sets: 50, deletes: 3, errors: 0, latency: { get: { p50, p95, p99 }, set: { p50, p95, p99 } } }
```

`cache.stats` is `undefined` when the plugin is not registered.

## Stale plugin

<Callout type="warn">
  `stalePlugin` is currently a stub — it passes values through without implementing stale-while-revalidate behavior. The `staleTtl` option is accepted but has no effect.
</Callout>

```ts
import { stalePlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/stale";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  plugins: [stalePlugin({ staleTtl: "1m" })],
});
```

## Dedupe plugin

Deduplicates concurrent `get` calls for the same key. When multiple callers read the same key simultaneously, only one request reaches the adapter — the rest await the same promise.

```ts
import { dedupePlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/dedupe";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  plugins: [dedupePlugin()],
});
```

The in-flight map is module-level — shared across all `createCache` instances that use this plugin.

## Prefetch plugin

Warms the cache for related keys whenever a key is read. Supply a `keyFn` that returns the related keys to prefetch for a given key:

```ts
import { prefetchPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/prefetch";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  plugins: [
    prefetchPlugin({
      keyFn: (key) => [`${key}:meta`, `${key}:tags`],
      maxKeys: 5, // max related keys to prefetch per get (default: 10)
    }),
  ],
});
```

Prefetched keys are fetched in the background — they do not block the current `get` and errors are silently ignored.

## Audit plugin

Fires callbacks on every cache operation. Use for logging, metrics, or compliance tracing:

```ts
import { auditPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/audit";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  plugins: [
    auditPlugin({
      onGet: (key, hit) => console.log(`GET ${key} — ${hit ? "HIT" : "MISS"}`),
      onSet: (key) => console.log(`SET ${key}`),
      onDelete: (key) => console.log(`DELETE ${key}`),
    }),
  ],
});
```

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `onGet` | `(key, hit) => void \| Promise<void>` | Called after every `get`; `hit` is `true` if a value was found |
| `onSet` | `(key) => void \| Promise<void>` | Called after every `set` |
| `onDelete` | `(key) => void \| Promise<void>` | Called after every `delete` |

Callbacks may be async. Errors thrown inside callbacks are not caught by the plugin — handle them inside the callback if needed.
